title: [Latest] Global GLP-1 Receptor Agonist Market Size/Share Worth USD 95.3 Billion by 2035 at a 12.1% CAGR: Healthcare Foresights (Analysis, Outlook, Leaders, Report, Trends, Forecast, Segmentation, Growth Rate, Value, SWOT Analysis)
author: Healthcare Foresights
description: The global GLP-1 receptor agonist market, valued at USD 28.7 billion in 2025, is projected to grow to USD 95.3 billion by 2035 at a 12.1% CAGR. Growth is driven by rising type 2 diabetes and obesity cases, technological innovations including oral formulations, and expanded insurance coverage. North America leads the market while Asia Pacific shows the highest growth rate.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key market player in the rapidly growing GLP-1 receptor agonist market with strong market presence and expected continued growth through 2035.
Key market player with recent positive development: advanced orforglipron (oral GLP-1 agonist) with positive Phase 3 results in December 2025, positioning for potential 2026 approval and launch.
Identified as a key market player in the expanding GLP-1 receptor agonist sector with strong growth prospects.

title: 3 Under-the-Radar Stocks to Buy and Hold for a Decade
author: Prosper Junior Bakiny
description: The article highlights three biotech companies with strong growth potential over the next decade: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals, which has the first-approved MASH treatment with significant market opportunity; Axsome Therapeutics, which is expanding its depression medicine Auvelity into Alzheimer's disease treatment; and Kailera Therapeutics, a newly public company developing weight-loss medicines in a fast-growing market. All three operate in high-need therapeutic areas with promising pipelines.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: First-mover advantage with FDA-approved MASH treatment (Rezdiffra), strong revenue growth (127% YoY), large addressable market of 315,000 patients, and promising pipeline. Risks include competition from Novo Nordisk and regulatory setbacks.

Strong revenue growth (57% YoY), Auvelity showing robust sales with new Alzheimer's disease agitation indication, projected peak sales of $8 billion, and multiple pipeline candidates in development stages.

Recently public company with promising pipeline in fast-growing weight-loss market, leading candidate ribupatide in phase 3 trials with dual hormone mechanism potentially superior to existing GLP-1 drugs. Higher risk due to lack of approved products but significant upside potential.
Mentioned as established competitor in MASH market with approved treatment, representing competitive threat to Madrigal Pharmaceuticals but not the focus of the article.
Mentioned as established leader in weight-loss medicine market alongside Novo Nordisk, providing context for competitive landscape but not the focus of the article.

title: Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy Pill Turns Pricing Pain Into a Volume Bet
author: Itai Smidt
description: Novo Nordisk's stock has collapsed 67% from its June 2024 peak to the mid-$40s due to U.S. pricing pressures, competition, and its own decision to price the Wegovy pill at just $149/month as a volume-over-price strategy. While Q1 results showed 22% obesity-care growth and the company raised guidance, the market remains skeptical about whether the pricing power will recover. The stock trades near multi-year lows with scattered analyst targets ranging from $40 to $175, reflecting fundamental disagreement on the turnaround thesis. Management's DKK 15 billion buyback signals conviction, but Eli Lilly's competitive advantage and policy headwinds continue to weigh on sentiment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock shows signs of stabilization with Q1 beat and raised guidance, plus UK pill approval and upcoming Medicare coverage expansion. However, severe 67% de-rating, ongoing pricing collapse, CagriSema pipeline disappointment, and competitive disadvantage versus Eli Lilly create significant uncertainty. Management buyback shows conviction but fundamentals remain contested.

Decisively winning the narrative war with tirzepatide gaining market share and next-generation retatrutide positioned as a potential 'knockout punch.' Portfolio diversification across oncology and gene therapies reduces GLP-1 dependency. Market has consistently favored Lilly over Novo throughout the year.

title: 3 Reasons to Buy Eli Lilly Stock Like There's No Tomorrow
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: Eli Lilly is a leader in the GLP-1 weight-loss drug market with exceptional sales growth for Mounjaro and Zepbound. Despite a high P/E ratio of 40x, the company is growing into its valuation. Eli Lilly continues to innovate with GLP-1 pill versions and is strategically acquiring companies in infectious disease to build a diversified portfolio beyond GLP-1 drugs as patent protection eventually expires.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong sales growth (Mounjaro +99% in 2025, Zepbound +175%), market leadership in GLP-1 space, continued innovation with pill formulations, and strategic acquisitions to diversify beyond GLP-1 drugs position the company well for long-term growth despite current high valuation.

While Novo Nordisk was first to market with GLP-1 drugs (Wegovy) and its pill version is more effective, Eli Lilly has overtaken it in market share. The company faces competition but maintains a presence in the market with differentiated offerings.

title: Pfizer's New Weight-Loss Data Just Gave Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk Investors Something to Worry About
author: James Brumley
description: Pfizer's berobenatide, a GLP-1 receptor agonist anti-obesity drug, demonstrated comparable weight-loss results (15.9% at 32 weeks) to competitors but with a key advantage: monthly injections instead of weekly ones. With 10 trials in or entering phase 3 and 20+ anti-obesity treatments in development, Pfizer is positioning itself as a serious competitor to market leaders Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk. However, significant revenue contributions won't materialize until 2029, and increased competition will likely erode pricing power for existing weight-loss drugs.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Pfizer's berobenatide shows promising efficacy with a patient-friendly monthly injection advantage over competitors. The company has multiple trials in advanced stages and a robust pipeline of 20+ anti-obesity treatments, positioning it as an emerging competitor in a high-growth market.

Increased competition from Pfizer and other entrants will erode Eli Lilly's pricing power and market share in the weight-loss drug segment. The company's premium valuation has been largely dependent on its weight-loss drug potential, which is now threatened by viable alternatives.

Similar to Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk faces competitive pressure from Pfizer's berobenatide and other emerging alternatives. Despite current market dominance with $30B+ in anti-obesity drug revenue, the company's pricing power and growth prospects are at risk as competition intensifies.
